title: erigeron strigosus var. septentrionalis
intro: Erigeron strigosus var. septentrionalis, also known as the northern fleabane, is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the northern United States and Canada. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Erigeron strigosus var. septentrionalis include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve coughs and colds, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
